title: Add exhaustive switch checking for Protocol union — TypeScript must catch missing cases
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:59-68,2451,2578,2692 — The Protocol union has 9 members but all 3 switch statements have default branches, so adding a new protocol (e.g., 'udp') produces ZERO compiler errors despite 3 missing switch cases. Fix: remove default from messages/columns/canvas switches and add assertNever(x: never) exhaustive check. This forces TypeScript to flag every switch that doesn't handle the new protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a new member to Protocol union produces compiler errors at all 3 switch locations
assertNever helper function defined and used
Existing runtime behavior unchanged for all 9 protocols

title: Create shared ProtocolTimelineEvent base interface for all engine event types
description: 10 engine files define protocol events with overlapping fields (tick, from, to, description) but no shared contract. TCPEvent, TLSMessage, DNSQuery, WebSocketEvent, CORSStep, CDNStep, HTTPRequest all independently declare similar fields. Fix: create ProtocolTimelineEvent base interface in a shared-types.ts file. Each engine extends it with protocol-specific fields. This enables generic converters and guarantees SequenceDiagram compatibility.
acceptanceCriteria: ProtocolTimelineEvent base interface with tick, from, to, description
All 7 protocol event types extend ProtocolTimelineEvent
CORSStep gains tick and from/to fields (currently missing)
Conversion to SequenceMessage can use generic function accepting ProtocolTimelineEvent

title: Eliminate duplicate simulation execution — pass computed data from hook to visualization components
description: Every visualization component independently re-runs its simulation engine: DNS (hook line 2390 vs DNSVisualization:569), WebSocket (hook:2402 vs WSViz:853), CORS (hook:2415 vs CORSViz:1001), CDN (hook:2422 vs CDNViz:1135), HTTP (hook:2396 vs HTTPViz:636). Two separate simulator instances produce potentially divergent results (see DNS tick counter divergence). Fix: compute data once in the hook and pass as props.
acceptanceCriteria: Each simulation engine runs exactly once per configuration change
Visualization components receive computed event data as props
Zero duplicate useMemo calls to simulation functions
Properties panel and canvas show identical data

title: Create protocol registry pattern — single object per protocol with all required pieces
description: Adding a new protocol currently requires finding 5-8 files with zero compiler guidance. Fix: create a ProtocolRegistration<E> interface that requires: id, name, description, columns, simulate(), toSequenceMessages(), Visualization component, and optional Properties controls. Register each protocol once. The messages/columns/canvas switches become data-driven lookups instead of manual switch statements.
acceptanceCriteria: ProtocolRegistration interface defined with all required fields
Each of 9 protocols registered via the registry
Adding a protocol = adding one registration object (TypeScript guides all fields)
messages, columns, canvas derived from registry instead of switch statements

title: Enrich SequenceMessage with optional structured metadata (headers, encrypted, latency, success)
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:126-134 — SequenceMessage currently destroys all protocol-specific metadata during conversion. TLS loses encrypted/details, CDN loses latencyMs/headers, CORS loses headers/success. The Properties panel can only show label and description. Fix: add optional metadata Record, encrypted boolean, latencyMs number, success boolean to SequenceMessage.
acceptanceCriteria: SequenceMessage has optional metadata, encrypted, latencyMs, success fields
TLS converter preserves encrypted flag and details
CDN converter preserves headers and latency
CORS converter preserves headers and success status
Properties panel can display structured metadata when available

title: Constrain DNS and CDN from/to fields to typed string unions instead of bare string
description: dns-resolution.ts:33-35 uses string for from/to (should be DNSActor = 'client'|'recursive-resolver'|'root'|'tld'|'authoritative'). cdn-flow.ts:28-30 same issue (should be CDNActor = 'Client'|'DNS'|'Edge POP'|'Origin Shield'|'Origin'). Bare string means typos compile fine. Fix: define actor union types and use them.
acceptanceCriteria: DNSActor type constrains all DNS event from/to fields
CDNActor type constrains all CDN step from/to fields
Existing code compiles without changes (current strings already match unions)
A typo like 'recrsive-resolver' would now produce a compile error

title: Add tick and from/to fields to CORSStep — remove fragile string matching in converter
description: cors-simulator.ts:46-71 — CORSStep is the only protocol event type missing tick, from, and to. The converter at NetworkingModule.tsx:2480-2488 uses step.type.includes('response') to synthesize from/to, which will break for new step types. Fix: add tick (auto-incrementing in simulateCORS), from, and to fields to CORSStep.
acceptanceCriteria: CORSStep has tick, from, and to fields like all other protocol events
from/to values are 'Browser' or 'Server' based on step type
The messages converter no longer uses string matching for from/to
CORSStep extends ProtocolTimelineEvent (if NET-147 is done first)

title: Replace 10 useState calls in useNetworkingModule with useReducer for atomic state updates
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:2354-2366 — 10 useState calls manage protocol selection and parameters. Every handler (handleSelect, handleDnsScenarioChange, handleCorsConfigChange, etc.) manually pairs a state setter with setStepIndex(0). If a developer adds a new parameter and forgets to reset stepIndex, UI shows stale position. Fix: consolidate into useReducer with typed actions that atomically reset stepIndex.
acceptanceCriteria: Single useReducer replaces 10 useState calls
NetworkingState interface defined with all fields
Typed actions: SELECT_PROTOCOL, CHANGE_DNS_SCENARIO, etc.
Every action that changes protocol data automatically resets stepIndex
timerRef cleanup handled in reducer middleware or effect

title: Unify converter function naming pattern — all as protocolToSequenceMessages()
description: 9 converter functions use 5 naming patterns: tcpEventsToMessages, tlsToMessages, tls13ToSequenceMessages, tls13DataToMessages, dnsToSequenceMessages, dnsToMessages, wsToMessages, cdnToSequenceMessages, cdnToMessages. Fix: standardize as tcpToSequenceMessages(), tlsToSequenceMessages(), etc. Remove wrapper functions that add nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: All converters follow {protocol}ToSequenceMessages() pattern
Wrapper functions (dnsToMessages, cdnToMessages) eliminated
All call sites updated

title: Make TLS13Message extend TLSMessage — eliminate duplicate field declarations
description: tls-handshake.ts:41 and tls13-handshake.ts:39 — TLS13Message has the same 7 fields as TLSMessage plus rttPhase. Comment says 'Extends the base sequence format' but TypeScript does not actually extend. Fix: TLS13Message extends TLSMessage with added rttPhase field.
acceptanceCriteria: TLS13Message extends TLSMessage
rttPhase is the only additional field
Existing code using TLS13Message compiles unchanged
TLSMessage type updated if needed for TLS 1.2 types (NET-017)

title: Remove CDNSequenceMessage duplicate — use SequenceMessage directly
description: cdn-flow.ts:60-66 — CDNSequenceMessage is structurally identical to SequenceMessage minus fromState/toState. It should not exist as a separate type. Fix: import SequenceMessage (after it's exported from a shared location) or use Pick<SequenceMessage, 'from'|'to'|'label'|'description'|'highlighted'>.
acceptanceCriteria: CDNSequenceMessage type removed
cdnToSequenceMessages returns SequenceMessage[] or a compatible type
No functional regression

title: Move color values out of engine files into visualization layer
description: serialization-comparison.ts:44 has color field, tls13-handshake.ts:442 returns hex colors, dns-resolution.ts:167 returns hex colors, cdn-flow.ts:199 returns hex colors. Engine files should return semantic identifiers; the visualization layer maps to colors. Fix: engines return state names ('encrypted','plaintext','cache-hit','cache-miss'), components map to colors via a shared color-map constant.
acceptanceCriteria: SerializationResult loses color field — mapped in component
tls13RowBackground returns state name not hex
dnsRowBackground returns state name not hex
cdnRowBackground returns state name not hex
Shared PROTOCOL_STATE_COLORS map defined in one place
